answer:
\boxed{B}

Napoleon’s diary entries reveal his authoritarian tendencies, particularly his control over the press (e.g., *"I shall never allow the newspapers to say anything contrary to my interests"*). His suppression of free speech and censorship of dissenting voices were central to his domestic policies, earning criticism for curtailing citizens' rights. While other options (like financial mismanagement or favoritism) were concerns, the most consistent and widely criticized issue was his erosion of civil liberties.
